operating_state: Consolidated revenues grew 9% to $70.2 billion driven by 1.1 million additional people served domestically through Medicare Advantage and Medicaid growth, partially offset by commercial enrollment declines from COVID-19 unemployment impacts. UnitedHealthcare revenues increased 8% while Optum revenues grew 11% from organic expansion and acquisitions. Overall care activity approached seasonal baselines with temporary elective care deferrals offset by COVID-19 related care and testing costs.
cost_structure: Medical care ratio remained stable at 80.9% as temporary care deferrals and favorable reserve development offset COVID-19 care costs and permanent Health Insurance Tax repeal impacts. Operating cost ratio improved 90 basis points to 14.6% primarily from the Health Insurance Tax repeal and operating efficiency gains. Consolidated operating margin expanded 180 basis points to 9.6%.
material_events: Andrew Witty became CEO and board member while Dirk McMahon was appointed President and COO, with David Wichmann retiring. The company announced definitive agreement to acquire Change Healthcare for approximately $12 billion, expected to close in second half 2021 subject to regulatory approvals. Board reduced shareholder threshold for special meetings from 25% to 15% and repurchased 5 million shares.
macro_exposures: COVID-19 pandemic continues creating uncertainty around care pattern normalization, with expectations that increased consumer demand for potentially higher acuity care and continued COVID-19 costs will drive medical cost increases in second half of year. Medicare Advantage 2022 rate increases of 4.1% fell short of forward medical cost trends, requiring continued cost management through coordinated care models.
forward_outlook: Management expects COVID-19 related care costs and economic impacts to be only partially offset by temporary care deferrals in second half of year as health systems return to seasonally adjusted levels. The company maintains quarterly dividend rate and continues seeking balance between growth and profitability. Pending Change Healthcare acquisition represents significant strategic expansion requiring substantial capital deployment.
uncertainty_profile: Ultimate COVID-19 impact remains uncertain depending on pandemic pacing and general economic conditions, with potential for increased medical cost reserve development variability. Regulatory uncertainties include ongoing DOJ legal action relating to risk adjustment submissions and potential changes in Medicare star ratings program application.
